title: thelychiton × nitidus
intro: Thelychiton × nitidus, also known as the Queensland stinging tree, is a plant species native to eastern Australia that produces highly toxic and irritating sap. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits associated with thelychiton × nitidus include potential uses in treating wounds, pain, and fever, although its toxicity requires careful handling and preparation. In herbal preparations, the sap is often diluted and used topically or applied to specific areas under the guidance of a medical professional.
